---
title: "bigwig_analysis"
author: "Daniel Arango"
date: "2/9/2025"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


```{r bigwig, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE, fig.width=4, fig.height=3, fig.path='R/', dev=c('png', 'pdf')}

# Load required libraries
library(rtracklayer)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)


# List of BigWig file paths
samples <- list(
  P_5_1 = "P51.bw",
  P_5_2 = "P52.bw",
  H_5_1 = "H51.bw",
  H_5_2 = "H52.bw"
)

# Define the chromosomes and position of interest
chromosomes <- c("5ETS", "A0", "A1", "18S", "E", "A2", "B1", "5_8S", "ITS2", "28S", "3ETS") 
position <- 35  # Position of interest
window_size <- 25  # Half-window size (±25)

# Initialize a data frame to store results
results <- data.frame(Sample = character(),
                      Chromosome = character(),
                      Mean_RPKM = numeric(),
                      stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Loop through each sample
for (sample_name in names(samples)) {
  # Import the BigWig file
  sample_data <- import(samples[[sample_name]], format = "BigWig")
  
  # Loop through each chromosome
  for (chr in chromosomes) {
    # Define the genomic range for this chromosome
    range <- GRanges(seqnames = chr,
                     ranges = IRanges(start = position - window_size,
                                      end = position + window_size))

    # Extract values for the range
    values <- subsetByOverlaps(sample_data, range)
    
    # Convert to data frame
    df <- as.data.frame(values)

    # Calculate mean RPKM if data exists, otherwise assign NA
    mean_rpkm <- ifelse(nrow(df) > 0, mean(df$score, na.rm = TRUE), NA)

    # Store results
    results <- rbind(results, data.frame(Sample = sample_name,
                                         Chromosome = chr,
                                         Mean_RPKM = mean_rpkm))
  }
}

# Print the final results
print(results)

# Convert results into wide format
results_wide <- results %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = Sample, values_from = Mean_RPKM)

# Print the transformed dataframe
print(results_wide)

# Rename Chromosome values for accuracy
results_wide <- results_wide %>%
  mutate(Chromosome = recode(Chromosome,
    "5ETS" = "5ETS",
    "A0" = "A_prime",
    "A1" = "A0",
    "18S" = "A1",
    "E" = "18S_end",
    "A2" = "E",
    "B1" = "A2",
    "5_8S" = "5S_start",
    "ITS2" = "ITS2",
    "28S" = "28S_start",
    "3ETS" = "3ETS"
  ))

results_wide <- results_wide %>%
  filter(Chromosome %in% c("A_prime", "A1"))

# Normalize each value by the sum of its respective column
results_wide <- results_wide %>%
  mutate(across(-Chromosome, ~ . / sum(., na.rm = TRUE), .names = "norm_{.col}"))

# Print updated dataframe
print(results_wide)

# compute the -log2 fold change
results_wide$rel.P5_1 <- results_wide$norm_P_5_1 / results_wide$norm_P_5_1
results_wide$rel.P5_2 <- results_wide$norm_P_5_2 / results_wide$norm_P_5_2
results_wide$rel.H5_1 <- results_wide$norm_H_5_1 / results_wide$norm_P_5_1
results_wide$rel.H5_2 <- results_wide$norm_H_5_2 / results_wide$norm_P_5_2

# Reshape the data for plotting
plot_data <- results_wide %>%
  filter(Chromosome == "A_prime") %>%  # Select only the row corresponding to "A_prime"
  pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("rel."), names_to = "Sample", values_to = "rel.values") %>%
  mutate(Sample = recode(Sample, 
                         "rel.P5_1" = "P5",
                         "rel.P5_2" = "P5",
                         "rel.H5_1" = "H5",
                         "rel.H5_2" = "H5"))

# Create the bar plot with points directly on the bars in gray
my.plot <- ggbarplot(plot_data, 
                     x = "Sample", 
                     y = "rel.values",
                     add = "mean_se",
                     color = "Sample", 
                     fill = "Sample", 
                     palette = c("P5" = "black", "H5" = "red"),
                     position = position_dodge(0.8)) + 
  geom_point(aes(x = Sample, y = rel.values), 
             color = "gray", 
             position = position_dodge(0.8), 
             size = 2) +  # Adjust size of the points
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0)) + # Ensure y-axis starts at 0
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(size = 1),
    axis.ticks = element_line(size = 1)
  ) +
  ggtitle("Relative A_prime Values")  # Add your title here

my.plot

```
